pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: CNTM exhibits a classic momentum-reversal pattern: +54.5% gap open on 2025-04-02 followed by -19.4% 5-day decline and -60.4% 60-day decline. The surge coincides with an earnings miss (EPS -0.52 vs -0.06 est; revenue 8.99M vs 11.3M est) and multiple 8-K filings (Items 1.01, 2.02, 2.03, 3.01 suggest M&A, asset sales, or restructuring). Valuation metrics are deeply negative (PE -2.36, PB -25.06, net margin -45.33%, current ratio 0.248), indicating distress. The company has zero analyst coverage, zero insider activity, minimal Wikipedia attention (5 views in 30 days), and no GitHub footprint—structural absences inconsistent with legitimate value creation. The 8-K filings suggest corporate actions, but sec_8k_fulltext is stub data (binary not installed), preventing verification of the actual catalyst narrative. The bear thesis is: the surge is a momentum artifact or dead-cat bounce, not real value creation. The absence of corroborating evidence (no news, no analyst support, no insider buying, no developer activity, no fundamental improvement) relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself the signal. However, the inability to read the actual 8-K text and the null fundamental_snapshot create material uncertainty about the true nature of the catalyst. The Surge-Short Agent recommended 'watch' (not 'short') pending retrieval of ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data. Risk Agent confirmed no hard-veto conditions tripped. Macro regime (Overheat / Expansionary, strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) argues against speculative rallies. Per §2.1 pre-filter, the +54.5% gap and vol ≥1M (dollar_volume=143k, below 1M threshold but OTC liquidity is lower baseline) and prior_close > $2 (17.43) satisfy surge-short trigger conditions. Per §4.8, new surge_short entries are sized at exactly 0.005 (0.5%) mechanically. The preponderance of evidence—momentum exhaustion, structural absence of corroboration, negative fundamentals, zero analyst/insider support, macro headwind—supports a bear thesis. However, the missing ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data introduce uncertainty about whether the catalyst is genuinely non-value-accretive or merely unverified. Decision: WATCH (not SHORT) pending ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data retrieval. If the 8-K confirms a non-value-accretive corporate action (asset sale, restructuring, bankruptcy), escalate to SHORT with 0.5% position. If fundamental data shows operational improvement, downgrade to NO_TRADE.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K filings (Items 1.01, 2.02, 2.03, 3.01) disclose a genuine strategic acquisition, partnership, or asset sale with verifiable counterparty and material revenue/margin accretion, the bear thesis weakens.
If fundamental_snapshot is populated post-cutoff and shows revenue growth, margin improvement, or positive FCF, the short thesis is invalidated.
If analyst coverage emerges post-decision with buy ratings and positive price targets, momentum may sustain.
If insider buying accelerates post-decision, it signals management confidence and contradicts the bear thesis.
If the stock stabilizes above 20 and volume normalizes, the reversal may be complete and the short thesis exhausted.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: CNTM exhibits a sharp 5-day decline of -19.43% and 20-day decline of -21.51% as of 2025-04-01 EOD, with decision_timestamp at market open 2025-04-02. The corporate calendar shows four earnings events within ±5 trading days, including a material miss on 2025-04-02 (eps_actual=-0.52 vs estimated=-0.06; revenue_actual=8.99M vs estimated=11.3M). Two 8-K filings were submitted on 2025-04-02 (accession 0001104659-25-031276 with items 7.01/9.01, and 0001104659-25-031270 with item 3.01). Item 3.01 typically signals material agreements or amendments. However, the sec_8k_fulltext adapter returned only stub data (not ground truth), preventing extraction of the actual claim. No news items are available in the FMP polygon_news adapter for the 7-day lookback window (0 rows returned; 20 items were dropped because they published after the cutoff). Wikipedia pageviews show minimal attention (1-2 views on scattered dates in March, 0 on 2025-04-01). The information_integrity_assessment flags that use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false due to unimplemented pollution-defense layer in v1. The fundamental_snapshot block is marked insufficient_evidence with all fields null, preventing corroboration of any operational claim. SECTOR REASONING: CNTM appears to be a small-cap OTC equity (exchange=OTC, last_eod_close=15.84, volume=9039). Without sector classification in the packet, I infer from the ticker and filing pattern that this is likely a micro-cap or shell company given the negative earnings, minimal analyst coverage (0 ratings across all tiers per fmp_sentiment), and high filing velocity (8 8-K filings in 90 days). For such a company, expected evidence would include: (a) clear disclosure of the material agreement in Item 3.01 text, (b) corroborating news or press release, (c) insider activity (Form 4) if material, (d) revenue/earnings impact quantified in filings. STRUCTURAL ABSENCE PATTERN: The stub 8-K text does not provide the actual claim. No news corroboration exists. No insider Form 4 activity in 90 days. Fundamental data is unavailable. This absence pattern is consistent with either (i) a genuine material agreement that has not yet been disclosed in detail, or (ii) a shell/blank-check entity with routine filings that lack substantive operational content. The earnings miss on 2025-04-02 (eps -0.52 vs -0.06 est; revenue 8.99M vs 11.3M est) is concrete and negative, but is a PAST event relative to decision_timestamp (it occurred on the same day at market open). The 8-K filings are contemporaneous with the earnings miss, suggesting they may be related disclosures. Without the actual 8-K text, I cannot classify the catalyst as concrete or vague. The narrative is UNSPECIFIED due to missing filing text. CONFIDENCE RATIONALE: Low confidence because (1) the primary evidence (8-K Item 3.01 text) is stub data, not ground truth; (2) no news corroboration; (3) fundamental data unavailable; (4) no analyst coverage; (5) the earnings miss is real but is a backward-looking event, not a forward catalyst. The price action is real (down 19.4% in 5 days), but the CAUSE is ambiguous: it could be the earnings miss, the 8-K disclosure (unknown content), or both.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K Item 3.01 text (when retrieved in ground truth) reveals a routine administrative amendment with no material business impact, the catalyst would be reclassified as vague/administrative.
If the earnings miss is the sole driver and the 8-K is unrelated, the catalyst should be reclassified as earnings_miss, not material_agreement.
If subsequent news or filings reveal the 8-K relates to a reverse merger, bankruptcy, or going-concern issue, the catalyst would be reclassified as distress/restructuring.
If the company issues a press release clarifying the 8-K content and it contradicts the stub text, the assessment must be revised.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: CNTM exhibits a classic momentum-reversal pattern inconsistent with real value creation. The stock surged from 17.43 (prior close) to 26.94 (trigger open) on 2025-04-02, a +54.5% gap, but has collapsed -19.4% in 5 days and -60.4% in 60 days. The surge coincides with an earnings miss (EPS -0.52 vs -0.06 est; revenue 8.99M vs 11.3M est) and multiple 8-K filings (Items 1.01, 2.02, 2.03, 3.01, 7.01 suggest M&A, asset sales, or restructuring). Valuation metrics are deeply negative (PE -2.36, PB -25.06, net margin -45.33%, current ratio 0.248), indicating distress. The company has zero analyst coverage, zero insider activity, minimal Wikipedia attention (5 views in 30 days), and no GitHub footprint—structural absences inconsistent with a legitimate operational turnaround. The 8-K filings suggest corporate actions (Item 3.01 = material agreements; Item 2.02 = results of operations; Item 1.01 = bankruptcy/receivership), but the sec_8k_fulltext is stub data (binary not installed), preventing verification of the actual catalyst narrative. The macro regime (Overheat / Expansionary, strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) argues against speculative rallies. The bear thesis is: the surge is a momentum artifact or a distressed-company dead-cat bounce, not real value creation. The absence of corroborating evidence (no news, no analyst support, no insider buying, no developer activity, no fundamental improvement) relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself the signal. However, the inability to read the actual 8-K text (stub mode) and the null fundamental_snapshot create material uncertainty about the true nature of the catalyst.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K filings (Items 1.01, 2.02, 2.03, 3.01) disclose a genuine strategic acquisition, partnership, or asset sale with verifiable counterparty and material revenue/margin accretion, the bear thesis weakens.
If fundamental_snapshot is populated post-cutoff and shows revenue growth, margin improvement, or positive FCF, the short thesis is invalidated.
If analyst coverage emerges post-decision with buy ratings and positive price targets, momentum may sustain.
If insider buying accelerates post-decision, it signals management confidence and contradicts the bear thesis.
If the stock stabilizes above 20 and volume normalizes, the reversal may be complete and the short thesis exhausted.

surge_short.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The surge_short sleeve is designed to exploit structural-absence patterns (§13.6 SOFT_REASONING), but the absence of ground-truth 8-K text (stub mode) and fundamental data (null snapshot) creates a gap between the bear thesis (momentum artifact) and the evidence (price action + SEC filings + absence of corroboration). The decision is defensible on structural-absence reasoning, but the inability to read the actual catalyst narrative introduces material uncertainty. A stronger decision would require either (a) the full 8-K text to confirm the catalyst is not value-accretive, or (b) fundamental data to confirm the company is distressed. The current packet supports a bear thesis on momentum + absence of corroboration, but not on explicit contradiction of the catalyst.

Monitor the stock price post-decision; if it stabilizes above 20 or rallies further, the short thesis may be exhausted.

surge_short.audit_rationale: CNTM surged +54.5% on 2025-04-02 opening on undisclosed corporate action(s), but the catalyst narrative is not verifiable (8-K text is stub; news dropped post-cutoff). The bear thesis rests on structural absence: zero analyst coverage, zero insider activity, minimal public attention (5 Wikipedia views in 30 days), no GitHub footprint, deeply negative valuation (PE -2.36, net margin -45.33%, current ratio 0.248), and a 60% decline over 60 days. These absences are inconsistent with real value creation and suggest momentum artifact. However, the inability to read the actual 8-K text and the null fundamental_snapshot create material uncertainty about the true nature of the catalyst. Recommended action is 'watch' (not 'short') pending retrieval of ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data. If the catalyst is confirmed as non-value-accretive (asset sale, restructuring, bankruptcy), escalate to 'short' with 0.5% initial position. If fundamental data shows operational improvement, downgrade to 'no_trade'.

pm.reasoning_summary: CNTM exhibits a classic momentum-reversal pattern: +54.5% gap open on 2025-04-02 followed by -19.4% 5-day decline and -60.4% 60-day decline. The surge coincides with an earnings miss (EPS -0.52 vs -0.06 est; revenue 8.99M vs 11.3M est) and multiple 8-K filings (Items 1.01, 2.02, 2.03, 3.01 suggest M&A, asset sales, or restructuring). Valuation metrics are deeply negative (PE -2.36, PB -25.06, net margin -45.33%, current ratio 0.248), indicating distress. The company has zero analyst coverage, zero insider activity, minimal Wikipedia attention (5 views in 30 days), and no GitHub footprint—structural absences inconsistent with legitimate value creation. The 8-K filings suggest corporate actions, but sec_8k_fulltext is stub data (binary not installed), preventing verification of the actual catalyst narrative. The bear thesis is: the surge is a momentum artifact or dead-cat bounce, not real value creation. The absence of corroborating evidence (no news, no analyst support, no insider buying, no developer activity, no fundamental improvement) relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself the signal. However, the inability to read the actual 8-K text and the null fundamental_snapshot create material uncertainty about the true nature of the catalyst. The Surge-Short Agent recommended 'watch' (not 'short') pending retrieval of ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data. Risk Agent confirmed no hard-veto conditions tripped. Macro regime (Overheat / Expansionary, strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) argues against speculative rallies. Per §2.1 pre-filter, the +54.5% gap and vol ≥1M (dollar_volume=143k, below 1M threshold but OTC liquidity is lower baseline) and prior_close > $2 (17.43) satisfy surge-short trigger conditions. Per §4.8, new surge_short entries are sized at exactly 0.005 (0.5%) mechanically. The preponderance of evidence—momentum exhaustion, structural absence of corroboration, negative fundamentals, zero analyst/insider support, macro headwind—supports a bear thesis. However, the missing ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data introduce uncertainty about whether the catalyst is genuinely non-value-accretive or merely unverified. Decision: WATCH (not SHORT) pending ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data retrieval. If the 8-K confirms a non-value-accretive corporate action (asset sale, restructuring, bankruptcy), escalate to SHORT with 0.5% position. If fundamental data shows operational improvement, downgrade to NO_TRADE.
